Open a Colab notebook and set the runtime to use a T4 GPU accelerator
Import TensorFlow, Keras, KerasNLP, and other necessary libraries including random and numpy

Set up the English-to-Spanish translation example environment by cloning or downloading the KerasNLP neural machine translation example notebook from https://colab.research.google.com/github/keras-team/keras-io/blob/master/examples/nlp/ipynb/neural_machine_translation_with_keras_nlp.ipynb
Install the required keras-nlp package version compatible with Keras 3.2.1
Load or prepare the English-to-Spanish parallel dataset exactly as done in the example, including tokenization and vocabulary creation

Build the Transformer model architecture for translation according to the example code, including the encoder and decoder with the specified parameters (e.g., number of layers, embedding dimensions, number of heads)
Compile the model with the optimizer and loss function as shown in the tutorial
Train the model on the prepared dataset for at least one epoch to ensure the model trains successfully without errors

Define the decode_sequences function as in the example, which uses keras_nlp.samplers.GreedySampler to generate translations, passing the 'end_token_id' keyword argument to the sampler's __call__ method
Within the decode_sequences function, create the prompt by concatenating the start token and padding tokens with the correct shapes and data types
Call the GreedySampler instance with the next token probability function and the prompt, explicitly passing the end_token_id argument (e.g., end_token_id=spanish_tokenizer.token_to_id('[END]'))
Run the decode_sequences function on at least two random input English sentences from the test set (e.g., by calling decode_sequences([input_sentence]) where input_sentence is a string)
Observe the runtime error: TypeError: Sampler.__call__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'end_token_id'
Capture the full stack trace showing that the error originates from the call to keras_nlp.samplers.GreedySampler().__call__ with end_token_id argument
Verify that the error consistently occurs with Keras version 3.2.1 and Python 3.10.12 in the Colab T4 GPU environment
Assert that the training phase completes successfully without errors, confirming that the bug only manifests during inference/decoding
Optionally, print the version of keras_nlp and keras to confirm environment matches conditions that trigger the bug
